channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dodoma patients receive support from Finance Ministries' annual outreach
item.description: In Dodoma, a total of 38 women admitted in the maternity ward and 44 children under five in the paediatric ward received humanitarian assistance from the Ministry of Finance in partnership with Zanzib...

item.title: Children Encounter Coded Predator Language Online Before Parents Recognize It
item.description: The article describes how children are increasingly encountering coded terms related to predatory behavior online, often before their parents are even aware of them. One mother discovered this when he...

item.title: Court of Appeal Defers Hearing in Consumer Suit Against P&G and Kimberly-Clark Over Product Labelling
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Kenya has postponed proceedings in a major consumer rights case involving more than 2,800 Kenyans against global companies Procter & Gamble and Kimberly-Clark. The case was file...

item.title: Drama Festival Bans Political Content, School Disqualified Over Singapore Reference
item.description: The 2026 Kenya National Drama and Film Festival has introduced strict new guidelines banning political messaging, following controversies over plays with political content in the past. The revised rul...

item.title: Artemis II crew viewed Ryan Gosling’s Project Hail Mary prior to historic lunar mission
item.description: Before embarking on their landmark lunar voyage, the Artemis II astronauts found a moment of relaxation by watching Ryan Gosling’s new science fiction film, *Project Hail Mary*. Jeremy Hansen, one of ...

item.title: Trump’s Strategy Toward Iran Reflects Nixon’s ‘Madman Theory’ Approach
item.description: The article examines how former U.S. President Donald Trump’s handling of the conflict with Iran may mirror Richard Nixon’s 'Madman Theory'—a Cold War tactic designed to keep adversaries uncertain by ...

item.title: Study reveals how psychedelics rewire the brain, showing common ‘neural fingerprint’
item.description: A large-scale international study has identified a common ‘neural fingerprint’ produced by psychedelic drugs such as LSD, psilocybin, DMT, mescaline, and ayahuasca in the brain. Researchers analysed o...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee criticises PM Modi for not responding to Pakistan Defence Minister’s remarks on Kolkata
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has publicly criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi for not responding to remarks made by Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Asif, who warned that Kolkata ...

item.title: Police helicopter with four aboard missing in Kruger National Park during animal carcass probe
item.description: A police helicopter carrying four people disappeared inside the Kruger National Park on Monday while on a routine operation. The aircraft was dispatched around 9am to investigate the discovery of an a...

item.title: PM Urges Tanzanians to Uphold Peace and Unity for Social and Economic Progress
item.description: Prime Minister Dr Mwigulu Nchemba has called on all Tanzanians to maintain peace and unity, noting that stability is essential for both social life and economic development. Speaking during an Easter ...

item.title: Rescue of Bald Eagles in B.C. After Getting Caught in Powerlines
item.description: A dramatic rescue was captured on video when two bald eagles were found tangled in powerlines near Vancouver, British Columbia. The incident took place in early April, and a group of wildlife rescuers...

item.title: Artemis II Crew Completes Historic Lunar Flyby with Record-Breaking Observations
item.description: The Artemis II mission marked the first crewed lunar flyby in over fifty years, setting new records and offering astronauts extraordinary views of the Moon and Earth. Commander Reid Wiseman, along wit...

item.title: Media groups urge President Tinubu to probe Wike’s alleged threat against Channels TV presenter Seun Okinbaloye
item.description: Some top media and rights organizations including di Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE), Nigeria Union of Journalists (NUJ) and Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don call on Presid...

item.title: Muturi Calls for Halt on Voter Register Updates Until Forensic Audit of Population Data is Completed
item.description: Former Attorney General Justin Muturi has urged the Kenyan government to immediately halt any modifications to the voter register, citing risks to its credibility unless a full forensic audit of the n...

item.title: Impact of Devolution on Household Welfare in Kenya's Marginalised Counties
item.description: Since Kenya devolved power to 47 counties in 2013, there has been a clear effort to reduce historical inequalities. The 2010 constitution ensured counties got a share of national revenue, with extra f...

item.title: Trump Claims US Weapons Sent to Support Iran Protesters Were Misused
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump talk say America try support Iranian protesters wey dey against their government, but the weapons wey dem send no reach the right people. Trump yarn this one for White...

item.title: Actor Portable Requests Custody of Son, Responds to Ex-Wife's Allegations
item.description: Nollywood actor Portable has publicly addressed his ex-wife Fatimo Momoh, requesting that she bring their 10-year-old son, Mahbub, to him. This follows Fatimo’s accusations that Portable abandoned the...

item.title: India supports diplomatic efforts to end regional conflict in West Asia
item.description: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar held discussions with his Iranian counterpart Seyed Abbas Araghchi, emphasising India's support for ongoing regional and international initiatives to halt the o...

item.title: National School Games Commence in Kisumu: Top Teams Face Off
item.description: The Brookside Secondary Schools Term One National Games officially kicked off today in Kisumu, bringing together over 2,000 students from across the country. Seven venues are buzzing with activity as ...

item.title: Government Launches Multi-Agency Security Camp to Combat Trafficking on Northern Border
item.description: The Kenyan government has started construction of a new multi-agency security camp in Eldas Constituency, near the border of Marsabit and Wajir counties. This move is aimed at tackling persistent huma...
